title: New hack exploits AI hallucinations to trick agents into running malicious code 'HalluSquatting' attack exploits a fundamental weakness in every available model
summary: Attackers can exploit AI hallucinations to trick agents into running malicious code, creating massive botnets.
insight: AI hallucinations leave models vulnerable to widespread exploitation everywhere.

title: Future Nostalgia Project asks retro hoarders to Copy That Floppy! flips the early 1990s anti-piracy campaign on its head to encourage budding archivists
summary: The Future Nostalgia Project reverses the early 1990s anti-piracy 'Copy That Floppy!' campaign by encouraging people to copy and preserve nostalgic floppy disks, promoting archiving and preservation of retro technology.
insight: Preserving digital history through community-driven archiving efforts matters deeply.

title: AMD EXPO ULL shows middling performance gains in initial tests despite eye-watering price increase first benchmarks show up to a 4% improvement with DDR5-6000 CL36
summary: AMD's EXPO ULL memory shows limited performance gains, up to 4%, despite significant price increase. Initial tests indicate that the higher cost does not translate to substantial performance benefits. This may impact consumer purchasing decisions.
insight: Middling performance gains outpace price increases in EXPO ULL memory

title: AI servers will consume more power than all conventional data center hardware combined by 2027 global data center electricity consumption set to grow by 26% this year, Gartner forecasts
summary: Global data center electricity consumption is expected to grow 26% in 2026 to reach 565 TWh, up from 447 TWh in 2025. This significant increase is forecasted by Gartner. The growth is driven by the expanding demand for cloud computing and AI services.
insight: Rising AI demand fuels explosive data center electricity growth.

title: Chinese courts allow heirs to inherit accounts of deceased gamers multiple cases spanning years establish precedent for digital ownership of games, in-game items, and microtransactions
summary: Chinese courts have allowed heirs to inherit digital assets, setting precedent for ownership rights. This decision is based on multiple rulings, despite Chinese law not explicitly covering digital properties. The verdicts have implications for digital ownership and inheritance.
insight: Digital assets gain recognition as inheritable, valuable property interests

title: Rapidus fab roadmap examined first new leading-edge chipmaker in decades has one Hokkaido fab, a 2027 deadline, and 60 potential customers
summary: Rapidus is building Japan's first leading-edge logic fab in Chitose, Hokkaido, with a 2027 deadline and 60 potential customers.
insight: Japan's return to leading-edge chip production after decades of absence.

title: China alleges that Claude Code contains backdoors, calls mechanism 'a serious threat' Gov't claims Claude sends sensitive information to remote servers without consent
summary: China warns users against using certain versions of Claude Code, claiming hidden code sends sensitive user information to remote servers without consent. The government advises uninstalling the app or using its latest version. Claude Code is not approved for use in China.
insight: Claude Code's secret data transfer poses significant national security risks.

title: Hidden backdoor in Tenda routers goes unpatched as company ignores warnings from cybersecurity researchers Chinese company's firmware allows admin access without a password
summary: A critical authentication backdoor in Tenda router firmware grants admin access without valid credentials, leaving users vulnerable. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-11405, affects multiple firmware versions and has no available patch. Researchers have warned Tenda, but the company has yet to address the issue.
insight: Unpatched backdoor exposes users to full administrator access risk

title: JEDEC releases new SPHBM4 standard to slash AI memory costs Narrow 512-bit interface enables dropping expensive interposers for organic substrates
summary: JEDEC releases SPHBM4, a new standard that achieves HBM4-class bandwidth without silicon interposers or CoWoS-like packaging, reducing costs for AI memory.
insight: Simplifies AI memory production, lowers costs significantly.

title: Global consumer Wi-Fi router shipments fell 6% in Q1 2026, down 34% from 2021 peak mesh systems and gaming routers still prove popular
summary: Global consumer Wi-Fi router shipments fell 6% in Q1 2026. This decline comes after a 34% drop from the 2021 peak. Despite this, mesh systems and gaming routers remain popular.
insight: Decline of Wi-Fi router shipments impacts network infrastructure and consumer technology.

title: SiPearl's long-awaited Rhea CPU finally gets in the lab, opening the door for Europe's first sovereign HPC CPU 'availability of Rhea1 is scheduled for end of 2026' SiPearl VP says, following long development process
summary: SiPearl's Rhea CPU marks a significant milestone for Europe's HPC ambitions. Following a long development process, the limited run CPU is expected to become available at the end of 2026. Its availability could pave the way for Europe's first HPC processors on global markets.
insight: Limited run CPU opens doors for Europe's HPC market entry

title: Power company hikes data center bills by 30%, cuts residential electricity costs by 1.3% Oregon approves change through POWER Act, pushes developments using more than 20 Megawatts of power to pay their fair share
summary: Oregon's POWER Act allows PGE to increase data center bills by 30%, while reducing residential costs by 1.3%. The move aims to ensure large data centers pay their fair share of electricity costs. The change is set to impact developments using over 20 Megawatts of power.
insight: Large data centers must pay their fair share of electricity costs

title: 'Slopfix' software team charges $10,000 a week to delete AI-generated code bloat ironically, the team uses AI agents to trim messy repositories by up to 65%
summary: Slopfix charges $10,000 a week to refactor AI-generated code, using AI agents to trim up to 65% of code bloat.
insight: AI-generated code often requires AI to make it manageable and efficient.

title: Fi Ultra becomes first dog tracker powered by Starlink satellites the Fi Ultra Dog Tracker makes Fido trackable via satellite, onboard GPS, and LTE connectivity
summary: Fi Ultra becomes the first dog tracker with Starlink satellite connectivity, onboard GPS, and LTE, making Fido trackable from anywhere.
insight: Enhanced global tracking capabilities for pet owners.

title: Cooler Master V4 and V8 3DHP Review: A masterful engineering achievement
summary: Cooler Master's V4 and V8 coolers feature innovative 3DHP heatpipes, a significant advancement in air cooling technology. This design offers improved heat dissipation and reduced noise levels. However, the early adoption of this technology comes at a higher price point.
insight: Revolutionary 3DHP heatpipe design redefines air cooling performance

title: Cracked version of Assassin s Creed Black Flag Resynced leaked days prior to official release despite Denuvo DRM protection Denuvo unable to stop crackers, with some finding ways to completely remove it from other titles
summary: A cracked version of Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced has leaked online, despite Denuvo DRM protection. The leak occurred more than a month before the official release date. This incident raises concerns about the effectiveness of Denuvo.
insight: Denuvo's failure to stop piracy undermines its anti-piracy value

title: Modding tool 'DLSS Swapper' might infect your PC with malware if you download the wrong files App creator warns against using random, user-submitted DLLs
summary: The creator of DLSS Swapper warns against using random, user-submitted DLLs due to malware risks. These files, even if hosted on the app's GitHub repo, may not be safe. Users are advised to only download official, verified files.
insight: Malware risks rise with user-submitted files, no matter the source.

title: Save $527 on this 1440p gaming PC from Newegg with a 16GB RTX 5060 Ti right now huge discount for high-end performance rig with an Intel Core i7-14700F, 32GB DDR5, and a 1TB SSD
summary: Newegg's ABS gaming PC boasts 1440p performance with a 16GB RTX 5060 Ti and 32GB DDR5 RAM.
insight: High-end performance at a discounted price for 1440p gaming enthusiasts.

title: Valve releases drivers, notes to make Windows work on Steam hardware, but refuses to support it tells users it doesn t offer support for Windows on Steam Hardware, gaming company provides resources as is
summary: Valve releases drivers to improve Windows 11 compatibility on Steam Deck/Machine, but refuses support, pointing users to SteamOS recovery instructions.
insight: Valve prioritizes SteamOS, limiting Windows support on Steam hardware.

title: 8Bitdo's wireless Pro 2 gaming controller falls to all-time low price hall-effect gamepad is 38% off, just $37.19
summary: The 8Bitdo wireless Pro 2 gaming controller is on sale, 38% off, for $37.19. This is an all-time low price at Amazon for Prime members. The Pro 2 features hall-effect joysticks for precision gaming.
insight: Gaming enthusiasts can upgrade their experience at a budget-friendly price.

title: Nvidia touts Vera CPU's single-threaded performance as its agentic AI advantage, reveals next-gen 'Rigel' Arm CPU cores frames chip as a 'max single-threaded CPU at scale,' not a parallel monster
summary: Nvidia touts Vera CPU's single-threaded performance as its agentic AI advantage, with 1.8x uplift in agentic workloads and 1.5x in coding compared to x86 competition.
insight: Nvidia prioritizes single-threaded performance in Vera CPU design for AI applications.

title: Makerfabs MaUWB for Home Assistant An ESP32-S3 UWB module with PoE and enclosure
summary: The Makerfabs MaUWB module is an ESP32-S3-based UWB module for indoor positioning and RTLS, integrating with Home Assistant via ESPHome for seamless tracking of tagged devices.
insight: Precise indoor location tracking for smart homes and offices enabled.

title: Norik Systems introduces nRF9151-based USB Dongle for DECT NR+ deployments
summary: Norik Systems introduced a compact USB dongle using Nordic's nRF9151 SiP for DECT NR+ deployments, enabling developers to easily integrate the non-cellular 5G standard into gateways or child nodes.
insight: Accelerating DECT NR+ adoption with easy-to-use, compact USB connectivity solutions

title: GIANT scientists deploy sensors in drones and robot subs to map Greenland s melting ice
summary: Scientists deploy sensors in drones and robots to map Greenland's melting ice, making research possible in treacherous waters.
insight: Melting ice affects global sea levels and climate stability.

title: Japanese firm reverses Blu-ray drive exit after securing parts, but prices jump up to 51% and supplies are limited
summary: Buffalo reversed its decision to discontinue its portable Blu-ray drives after securing necessary parts. However, the drives will now come with significantly higher prices and limited supplies.
insight: Supply constraints lead to price hikes for discontinued products.

title: Philips Evnia 27M2N5500XD 1000Hz Monitor Review
summary: The Philips Evnia 27M2N5500XD falls short of its advertised specs due to slow IPS response times.
insight: IPS response times hinder monitor's high refresh rate performance.

title: Meta's new AI tool can generate images of you using your Instagram photos, and you're opted in by default
summary: Meta's AI tool generates images using Instagram users' photos by default, without notification.
insight: Users' privacy and control over AI-generated images are compromised by default settings.

title: What was the first consumer device powered by a Li-ion battery?
summary: The first consumer device powered by a Li-ion battery was a handheld electronic calculator called the 'Intellect' in 1991, made by Molex. This device paved the way for widespread adoption of lithium-ion technology in consumer electronics. The Intellect was a significant innovation in battery technology.
insight: Early Li-ion adoption revolutionized portable electronics and changed tech history.

title: A new satellite wants to prove nuclear power can work in space without solar panels
summary: BOHR's mission tests a betavoltaic power source that converts beta particles into electricity, a potential nuclear power alternative for space missions without solar panels.
insight: Nuclear power in space could provide a reliable, long-lasting energy source.

title: This dog tracker uses Starlink to keep tabs on your pet, even without cell service
summary: Fi's Fi Ultra uses Starlink for satellite-based dog tracking coverage, even without cell service.
insight: Enhanced pet safety in remote areas with no cell connectivity.

title: Samsung unveils fastest PCIe Gen 6 SSD, capable of transferring 40GB LLMs in just 1.4 sec
summary: Samsung has unveiled its fastest PCIe Gen 6 SSD with up to 28.4 GB/s read speed and 21.9 GB/s write speed, enabling fast transfer of large data, such as a 40GB LLM in just 1.4 seconds.
insight: Accelerating AI model deployment with significantly improved data transfer speeds

title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced modernizes the fan favorite with revamped combat and visuals
summary: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced brings smoother combat and visual upgrades to the classic game, polishing its foundation while still showing some age.
insight: Revamping a classic game to modern standards is a significant industry shift.

title: Xbox Game Pass has lost 4 million subscribers since its price hike backfired
summary: Xbox Game Pass has lost 4 million subscribers since its price hike.
insight: Subscriber growth slowdown threatens flagship service's long-term success prospects.

title: Starlink just doubled its prices for private jets, and hardware now costs $200,000
summary: Starlink doubled private jet prices and increased hardware costs to $200,000.
insight: Higher costs may impact business aviation's adoption of Starlink services.

title: This tiny robot could help dentists prepare crowns in a single visit
summary: The MIR robot streamlines the crown preparation process for dentists and patients.
insight: Single-visit crowns reduce patient stress and increase treatment efficiency.

title: Cheyenne suspends datacenter wastewater after rare bacterium traced to Meta's AI site
summary: A rare bacterium in Cheyenne's wastewater was linked to Meta's AI data center construction. The bacterium, Cupriavidus gilardii, was traced to a contractor's discharge from the site. The data center, valued at $800 million, is expected to be operational in 2027.
insight: Rare bacterium contaminates water supply near Meta's massive AI data center

title: Nintendo to ship Switch 2 with easier-to-replace batteries in Europe later this year
summary: Nintendo's Switch 2 consoles in Europe will feature easier battery replacement post-2026.
insight: Compliance with EU regulations drives design changes.

title: Meta will disable your smart glasses' camera if you tamper with the recording light
summary: Meta will disable smart glasses' camera if tampered with, preventing unauthorized access.
insight: Tampering with recording light compromises wearable camera security and personal data.

title: Hardware hacker is building a homemade GPU from 64,000 tiny RISC-V chips
summary: A hardware hacker is building a homemade GPU from 64,000 RISC-V chips, scaled down to a 320x200 resolution.
insight: Scaling down technology enables innovative prototypes and new possibilities in hardware design.
